discussion: On 150 converted HotpotQA examples, Reflexion improved exact-match accuracy from 77.33% for the one-pass ReAct baseline to 99.33%, a gain of 22 percentage points. ReAct missed 34 examples, mostly because the first answer selected a wrong final entity or stopped before fully resolving the multi-hop relation. Reflexion reduced wrong_final_answer failures from 34 to 1 by using evaluator feedback and storing a next-step strategy in reflection memory before retrying. No incomplete_multi_hop or entity_drift failures remained in the final Reflexion run, but these are still important failure categories for manual review because they describe the errors Reflexion is designed to correct. The tradeoff was cost: Reflexion used 382,014 tokens versus 261,286 for ReAct and increased average latency from 3.06s to 4.24s per question. The average attempt count rose only to 1.2267, so most questions still completed in one pass while hard cases benefited from reflection. After the full-set run, the dataset was split into a 100-question development set and a 50-question held-out test set with no qid overlap. The intended protocol is to tune prompts and retry settings on data/hotpot_dev.json, then run the final benchmark on data/hotpot_test.json.
